channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Springville teen treatment center cited after staff harms teen
item.description: A Utah County treatment center was cited after a video allegedly showed a staff member striking a teenager while they were physically restrained.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service head DeJoy to step down after 5 years marked by pandemic, losses and cost cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Louis DeJoy, the head of the U.S. Postal Service, intends to step down, the federal agency said Tuesday, after a nearly five-year tenure marked by the coronavirus pandemic, surges in mail-in election ballots and efforts to stem losses through cost and service cuts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dozens of police recruits nationwide have died while training in last decade: probe
item.description: At least 29 recruits died during basic training at law enforcement academies around the country in the last decade, an AP investigation found.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration gives schools a deadline to end DEI programs or risk losing federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is giving America’s schools and universities two weeks to eliminate diversity initiatives or risk losing federal money, raising the stakes in the president’s fight against “wokeness” and sowing confusion as schools scramble to comply.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Southwest Airlines cutting hundreds of jobs
item.description: Southwest Airlines announced Monday that it will cut 1,750 jobs in late April in what will be the first round of layoffs in the company’s 53-year history.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico said Monday it's awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alleged 'Zizians' leader was once declared dead in California
item.description: LaSota was still alive, however, when state police officers found and arrested her Sunday in Allegany County, Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: New Mexico's US attorney says he resigned at President Trump's request
item.description: Monday morning, he confirmed that he was one of the Biden-nominated U.S. attorneys who were locked out of their work devices.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, NewsNation

item.title: Man traveled cross-country to burn home of ex-girlfriend's online love interest: Police
item.description: Authorities say the fire completely engulfed the two-story single-family home, resulting in a total loss and the deaths of two dogs.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, NewsNation

item.title: Former Cleveland kidnapping victims now helping others
item.description: "I felt like this is what I’m supposed to do," Amanda Berry, who hosts a local TV station's 'Missing' segments, said.
item.dc:creator: Cathleen O'Toole, NewsNation

item.title: Fugitive jailed after melting hospital bed rail to escape
item.description: A man who was wanted for allegedly trying to shoot a gas station employee last year is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge declined to block Elon Musk’s DOGE from gaining access to Education Department data on student borrowers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Delphi murders: Gull rules against defense motions seeking to reexamine Richard Allen's conviction
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. -- Special Judge Fran Gull denied recent motions from Richard Allen’s attorneys seeking to reexamine his conviction last year in the Delphi murders case. In an order dated Feb. 14, Gull denied the defense’s Verified Motion to Correct Error (filed on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security chief steps down over DOGE access to recipient data: Reports
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting leader stepped down from her role over requests from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to access recipient data, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: Columbine survivor Anne Marie Hochhalter dies decades after shooting
item.description: Anne Marie Hochhalter, a survivor of the Columbine High School mass shooting, died Sunday, her former principal, Frank DeAngelis, told Nexstar's KDVR on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: American detained in Russia released as officials work to set up potential Trump-Putin meeting
item.description: Kalob Byers, a 28-year-old U.S. citizen detained in Russia on drug smuggling charges, was freed ahead of talks between Washington and Moscow on ending the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Toronto plane crash: Audio shows officials reacting to 'upside down and burning' Delta jet on tarmac
item.description: Officials witnessed Delta Connection flight 4819 crash on the runway of Toronto Pearson Airport, air traffic control audio shows. The CRJ-900 jet also flipped upside down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, KTLA 5

item.title: How to file your taxes for free
item.description: Filing a tax return doesn't have to cost you money. While some may hire a tax preparation expert or purchase online software, millions of taxpayers can file state and federal returns for free. Here are some options to file your taxes for free before the April 15 deadline.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KTLA 5

item.title: Toronto plane crash timeline: Delta flight from Minneapolis flips upside down while attempting to land
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight from Minneapolis crashed and overturned while landing at Toronto Pearson Airport on Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Top Democrats question IRS on DOGE access to taxpayer data
item.description: Top Democrats in Congress want to know what Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) plans to do at the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) after multiple outlets reported the advisory unit sought access to sensitive IRS systems. Senate Finance Committee members Ron Wyden (D-Ore.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: 8 injured after Delta plane flipped over during landing incident in Canada
item.description: A Delta Air Lines plane has flipped upon arrival at Toronto’s Pearson Airport and ended up on its roof, injuring 18 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Shakira hospitalized, postpones concert
item.description: Shakira has been hospitalized just two days into her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour. The "Hips Don't Lie" singer said she suffered from an abominable condition that forced her to cancel her show in Lima, Peru. "I am very sad that I will not be able to take the stage today.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: More adults are wondering if they have autism: Tips to seek a diagnosis
item.description: More adults have sought insight on their own neurodiversity in the last decade — often after their children are diagnosed or after seeing social media posts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, ABC 4

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Investigators enter a highway tunnel in Wyoming where a fiery crash killed 2
item.description: GREEN RIVER, Wyo. (AP) — Investigators in Wyoming began sifting through the wreckage inside a highway tunnel Saturday after smoke, hazardous chemicals and structural concerns prevented them from determining how many people may have died in the fiery pileup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrats nervously wait on Trump response to court rulings
item.description: House Democrats howling over the flood of unilateral actions streaming from the White House are voicing confidence that the courts will provide a check on potential abuses of power. They’re less certain, however, about how President Trump will respond.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Justice Department fires 20 immigration judges from backlogged courts amid major government cuts
item.description: The Trump administration fired 20 immigration judges without explanation, a union official said Saturday amid sweeping moves to shrink the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The 2025 Oscars won't use this 'lovely' format for the acting awards
item.description: The format was revived for the 2024 show — but it won't be repeated in the categories honoring the on-screen talent.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Anger, chaos and confusion take hold as federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Workers across the country responded with anger and confusion Friday as they grappled with the Trump administration 's aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off probationary employees who have yet to qualify for civil service protec
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, BRIAN WITTE, MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Denny's to close dozens more restaurants in 2025
item.description: Denny's was already planning to close dozens of locations this year. Now it's planning to close even more.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4
